screenStatusLegend.exists: Route and basic view exist in apps/web.
screenStatusLegend.partial: Route exists but the flow needs additional overlays, states, layout, or backend contract.
screenStatusLegend.missing: The product flow requires this screen or overlay, but it is not represented as a route/component yet.

componentLegend.anchored: Component file exists in apps/web/src/lib/components or a routes/* page.
componentLegend.target: Component referenced in the blueprint that does NOT yet exist as a shared component and should be extracted/created.

interactions.state: Open command palette over current screen.
Submit query and render mixed results (datasets, objects, apps, dashboards).
Open contextual share modal for the active resource.
Persist current resource via contextual mutation hook.
Open branch list and switch resource context.
Issue credentials, store session, redirect to last route.
Create user, elect first admin during bootstrap, then redirect to login with success context.
Collect slug, display name, description, workspace slug.
Share resource with user/group and access level.

interactions.element: Row actions > Permissions / Resource details > Permissions
interactions.state: Inspect owner, inherited project roles, direct shares, and grant or revoke user/group access.
Open lineage centered on this dataset.
Open node settings, validation report and preview controls.
Persist DAG, schedule and retry policy.
Show run logs and node statuses.
Create app or app from template.
Collect release notes and publish app.
Open object detail with properties, links, actions.
Focus on type and show connected types.
Map dataset columns to object properties.
Trigger build of all downstream datasets.
Define virtual table over existing connector.
Render API explorer from OpenAPI spec.
Reload object database topology, table metrics, distributions, and index metadata.
Filter the storage inventory locally by role and text while retaining the selected table detail panel.
Navigate from a storage layer or selected table to the operational surface that owns it.

implementationNotes: Use this file as seed data for a future /ui-map route.
Each interaction should map to a typed action registry in the frontend.
Every backend_action must have permission gating, loading, success and error states.
Drawers should be used for contextual inspection; modals should be used for creation, confirmation and publish/share flows.
Tabs should preserve parent route context and may optionally sync active tab to query params.
Components marked '(target)' in the screens array do not exist as shared components yet — create them when the corresponding flow is implemented.
For backend.path with status='recommended' the frontend currently has no client; the path is a proposed contract to align UI work with backend planning.
uiStates are advisory but every backend_action interaction MUST handle at minimum: idle, loading, success, error, permission_denied.
